findings.evidence: rotateEquiv (line 250) sets invFun := rotateSymm, so the equality is rfl; transposeEquiv_symm_apply at Rectangle.lean:721 is the established parallel lemma.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp] theorem rotateEquiv_symm_apply (S : GridRectangleBetween x.rotate y.rotate) : (rotateEquiv x y).symm S = rotateSymm S := rfl`.
findings.issue: rotateEquiv has no symm characterization, unlike the precedent transposeEquiv_symm_apply (Rectangle.lean:720-723, @[simp]). rotateSymm is fully developed as the inverse, so the symm side of the equivalence should be characterized too.

findings.evidence: rotateEquiv (line 285) uses invFun := GridState.rotate with left/right_inv := GridState.rotate_rotate; Complex.lean:78-83 documents and proves the transpose analogue.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp] theorem rotateEquiv_symm {R n} : (GridChain.rotateEquiv R n).symm = GridChain.rotateEquiv R n` (proof analogous to transposeEquiv_symm).
findings.issue: GridChain.rotateEquiv lacks the symm lemma that its sibling GridChain.transposeEquiv carries (Complex.lean:80-83, @[simp]), whose stated purpose is to let the forward _single/_apply simp lemmas drive the inverse map. Built from the GridState.rotate involution, the chain equiv is genuinely involutive, so the inverse currently has no simp normal form.

summary: The API faithfully mirrors the transpose stack (private helper, hidden bodies, matching simp annotations) and is essentially complete. Two characteristic-API gaps remain relative to the file's own precedent: the inverse direction of both the rectangle and chain rotation equivalences is left uncharacterized, whereas the transpose siblings provide symm lemmas.
